title: Buttons
intro: Buttons carry the action hierarchy of every Gravitate surface. GraviButton is the standard — boolean color flags plus an antd v5 appearance axis — with IconButton and EditSaveButton covering the icon-only and inline-edit niches.

blocks.body:
`GraviButton` is the only button most surfaces need. It wraps antd's Button with Gravitate's color flags (`theme1`, `theme2`, `success`, `error`) and an `appearance` axis that maps to antd v5 variants — reach for it anywhere you'd reach for antd Button and let the flags carry hierarchy.

The supporting cast is narrow: `IconButton` is a circular, tooltip-wrapped icon button for toolbars and row actions; `EditSaveButton` is a two-state pencil/save toggle for inline-editable sections inside antd Forms. `BigButton` is legacy — its styles no longer ship in the package — keep it out of new prototypes.

blocks.title: GraviButton color flags
blocks.caption: Resting states: no flags (quiet outlined default), theme1 (solid brand primary), success (solid green), error (solid red), and the disabled treatment.

blocks.title: GraviButton props
blocks.intro: GraviButton extends antd ButtonProps — everything not listed here passes straight through (`loading`, `size`, `block`, `href`).

rows.notes: The label. GraviButton renders buttonText, not React children — always self-close the tag.
The primary CTA. Solid brand-primary button with token-driven hover and active states. One per surface.
antd primary color, solid. Secondary emphasis when theme1 is already taken.
Solid green. Affirmative commits — Apply, Confirm.
Solid red. Destructive actions — Delete, Remove.
Maps to antd's variant. Always write 'outlined' — the legacy 'outline' silently remaps to the tinted 'filled' variant.
An antd icon element, e.g. icon={<PlusOutlined />}. Never a string — strings render as literal text. Omit buttonText for an icon-only button.
Direct antd color override; beats the boolean flags (except theme1). Reach for flags first.
Wrapped with a Google Analytics event before your handler runs. Submit forms here — htmlType='submit' passes through but is banned by house rule (EditSaveButton is the lone exception).

blocks.title: Color flags
blocks.intro: One boolean flag per button. Flags are mutually exclusive in spirit — when several are set, error wins over success, which wins over theme2.

rows.name: default
rows.when: Neutral and secondary actions — Cancel, Back, Reset.

rows.name: theme1
rows.when: The single primary action on a surface.

rows.name: theme2
rows.when: antd-primary emphasis when theme1 is already spent on this surface.

rows.name: success
rows.when: Affirmative commits that close a workflow — Apply, Confirm.

blocks.title: GraviButton appearance axis
blocks.caption: The appearance values on a success-flagged button: solid (default when flagged), outlined, filled (tinted), dashed, and text. Each maps directly to an antd v5 variant.

blocks.title: Icons in GraviButton
blocks.caption: icon takes an antd icon element: paired with buttonText (left), or icon-only when buttonText is omitted (right — antd applies square icon-only sizing automatically).

blocks.title: Canonical GraviButton usage
blocks.note: Every example self-closes: the label travels through buttonText, never children.

blocks.title: Utility buttons — EditSaveButton and IconButton
blocks.caption: EditSaveButton's two states — pencil (editing={false}) and save (editing={true}) — beside IconButton's circular, tooltip-wrapped icon button.

blocks.title: EditSaveButton props
blocks.intro: A small two-state toggle for inline-editable sections. It must live inside an antd <Form> — the save state submits the form.

rows.notes: false renders the pencil button that fires onEdit. true renders the save button, which submits the enclosing antd Form via an internal htmlType submit.
Fires from the pencil state only. There is no onSave — handle persistence in the Form's onFinish.
Concatenated with no separator — include a leading space (' my-class') or it fuses with the base class.

blocks.title: IconButton props
blocks.intro: A circular antd button inside a Tooltip. The wrapper div bakes in right padding (pr-3) — account for it when spacing toolbar neighbors.

rows.notes: Tooltip text — bottom placement, 0.5 s enter delay. Required in practice: an unlabeled icon button is a usability hole.

items.why: It marks the single primary action — two primaries cancel each other out.

items.rule: Default (no flags) is the workhorse for everything secondary.
items.why: The quiet outlined treatment keeps dense control bars readable.

items.rule: Reserve error for destructive commitments and success for affirmative commits — never decoratively.
items.why: Color is the warning; dilute it and users stop reading it.

items.rule: Demote buttons inside dense chrome with appearance='text' or 'filled' rather than shrinking labels.
items.why: Variant changes preserve hit targets and legibility; tiny type does not.

items.rule: Icon-only buttons always get a tooltip — use IconButton or pass hover text.
items.why: An unlabeled glyph forces users to click to find out what it does.
GraviButton renders the buttonText prop. Children are not the contract and render nothing in older releases.
theme is not a prop, and GraviButton always sets antd's color/variant pair, which overrides type — both silently do nothing.
'outline' is a legacy alias that remaps to the tinted 'filled' variant — you get a different button than you asked for.
Explicit submits keep form wiring visible and survive the analytics-wrapped click path. EditSaveButton is the lone sanctioned exception.
icon is an antd ReactNode slot — a string renders as literal text inside the button.

items.title: buttonText, not children
items.detail: GraviButton renders the buttonText prop and self-closes. Current releases fall back to children, but earlier ones drop them silently — and every library-internal caller uses buttonText. Treat children as off-limits.

items.title: warning, theme3, and theme4 are inert
items.detail: ThemeVariants declares them, but the current package never maps them to a style — they render identical to a bare outlined default. Use theme1, theme2, success, or error.

items.title: Every click reports to Google Analytics
items.detail: onClick is wrapped with a ReactGA event built from the first route segment and the button's text content. Button labels become analytics labels — keep them clean and free of sensitive data.

items.title: EditSaveButton needs an antd Form around it
items.detail: The save state is an internal htmlType submit with no onSave callback. Outside a Form, saving does nothing — wire persistence in the Form's onFinish.

items.title: BigButton ships unstyled in 5.x
items.detail: The .big-button CSS was dropped from the package stylesheet, so it renders as a bare div. Legacy — build promotional tiles with CheckCard or a styled card instead.

items.title: IconButton bakes in spacing and delay
items.detail: The wrapper div carries pr-3 right padding and the tooltip waits 0.5 s at bottom placement. Don't add extra margin to its right; don't expect instant hover labels.
